title: UN chief says 'diplomatic avenues' available to stop war in Lebanon as Hezbollah claims 'direct clashes' with Israeli forces
subhead: On Saturday, Israel kept up strikes on Lebanon as Hezbollah claimed attacks against northern Israel. UN Chief Antonio Guterres urged the international community to 'step up your engagement, empower the Lebanese state' and support the army, which has committed to disarming Hezbollah.

title: 'We are fighting absolute evil': General Eyal Zamir's 'existential war' as Israeli army's chief of general staff
subhead: The military officer, 60, who took office on March 5 and does not hesitate to voice his disagreement with certain decisions made by Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government, enjoyed widespread popularity in a country where the army is the backbone of society.

title: France beats England 48-46 to claim back-to-back Six Nations titles
subhead: Louis Bielle-Biarrey scored four of the 13 tries in a barn-storming encounter in which the lead changed hands numerous times. Meanwhile, Wales tasted Six Nations success for the first time in 1,099 days after blowing away Italy 31-17 at Principality Stadium on Saturday.

title: Races to watch in France's 2026 municipal elections
subhead: Aside from major cities like Paris and Marseille, the local election results from smaller towns like Le Havre and Menton are expected to reveal early political trends ahead of the 2027 presidential and legislative contests.

title: The French wine producers saying 'oui' to alcohol-free options
subhead: Although it remains a niche market in France, non-alcoholic wine is growing, especially among younger consumers – to the point of attracting some renowned producers.

Citizen investigators dig into the Epstein files
subhead: Since the declassification of the Epstein files, countless internet users have been delving into this massive trove of emails, videos, official reports and personal diaries. This citizen investigation sometimes turns into obsession.

title: 'In 1979, the Iranian left played a major role in the rise of the Islamic Republic'
subhead: Philosopher Stéphanie Roza and political scientist Amirpasha Tavakkoli explain how, throughout the 20th century, the political turmoil in Iran, torn between modernity and anti-modernity, ultimately led to Islamist victory.

title: Benjamin Netanyahu, a prime minister without checks and balances
subhead: The strikes in Iran have further reduced the political space for opposition in Israel, even as concerns mount over a possible ground offensive in Lebanon. Today, only Donald Trump seems capable of influencing the Israeli leader.
